questionTextSource: Two vehicles approach one another at an intersection. Both vehicles want to turn left. In what exceptional case do they have to turn one after the other?
explanationSource: Always turn in front of each other. If the traffic situation or the design of the intersection, e.g. lane markings, requires it, you must turn one behind the other.
answerASource: If the road traffic situation demands it
answerBSource: If the layout of the intersection demands it
answerCSource: If the vehicles are crossing over an equal priority intersection
questionTextTarget: Ikki transport vositasi chorrahada bir-biriga yaqinlashmoqda. Ikkalasi ham chapga burilmoqchi. Qaysi istisno holatda ular bir-birining ortidan burilishi kerak?
explanationTarget: Odatda har doim bir-birining oldidan buriladi. Agar yo‘l harakati vaziyati yoki chorrahaning tuzilishi, masalan yo‘lak chiziqlari, talab qilsa, bir-birining ortidan burilish kerak.
answerATarget: Agar yo‘l harakati vaziyati shuni talab qilsa
answerBTarget: Agar chorrahaning tuzilishi shuni talab qilsa
answerCTarget: Agar transport vositalari teng ustunlikdagi chorrahadan o‘tayotgan bo‘lsa

questionTextSource: Two vehicles approach one another at an intersection. Both vehicles want to turn left. What must both drivers do in the normal case?
explanationSource: If two left-turning vehicles come towards each other, they turn in front of each other (tangentially).
answerASource: - turn in front of one another
answerBSource: - turn behind one another
answerCSource: - communicate with one another to agree the mode of turning
questionTextTarget: Ikki transport vositasi chorrahada bir-biriga yaqinlashmoqda. Ikkalasi ham chapga burilmoqchi. Odatdagi holatda ikkala haydovchi nima qilishi kerak?
explanationTarget: Agar chapga burilmoqchi bo‘lgan ikki transport vositasi bir-biriga qarab kelsa, ular bir-birining oldidan, ya’ni tangensial tarzda buriladi.
answerCTarget: - burilish tartibini kelishish uchun bir-biri bilan aloqa qilish

questionTextSource: You want to turn left. What driving line should you maintain?
explanationSource: Use the right-hand driving line. The left-hand driving line is intended for oncoming traffic.
answerBSource: The left driving line
questionTextTarget: Siz chapga burilmoqchisiz. Qaysi harakat chizig‘ini saqlashingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: O‘ng tomondagi harakat chizig‘idan foydalaning. Chap tomondagi harakat chizig‘i qarama-qarshi transport uchun mo‘ljallangan.

questionTextSource: To which road user must you give way here?
explanationSource:
When turning, you must give priority to oncoming traffic and cross traffic - in this case cyclists and pedestrians.
Although your traffic lights are green, you must wait until the pedestrian has crossed the road. Pedestrians have priority over turning vehicles.
answerBSource: [The cyclist]
questionTextTarget: Bu yerda qaysi yo‘l harakati ishtirokchisiga yo‘l berishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Burilayotganda siz qarama-qarshi va kesishuvchi harakatga — bu holatda velosipedchilar va piyodalarga — ustunlik berishingiz kerak. Svetoforingiz yashil bo‘lsa ham, piyoda yo‘lni kesib o‘tguncha kutishingiz kerak. Piyodalar burilayotgan transport vositalaridan ustunlikka ega.

questionTextSource: You want to leave the roundabout at this exit. What is the right thing to do?
explanationSource:
When leaving the roundabout, crossing walkers and cyclists have priority.
You must therefore allow cyclists and walkers to cross the carriageway.
answerASource: I must allow [the pedestrian] to cross the road
answerBSource: I must allow [the cyclist] to cross the road
answerCSource: I have priority over the road users crossing the road
questionTextTarget: Siz aylanma yo‘ldan shu chiqishda chiqmoqchisiz. To‘g‘ri harakat qanday?
explanationTarget: Aylanma yo‘ldan chiqayotganda yo‘lni kesib o‘tayotgan piyodalar va velosipedchilar ustunlikka ega. Shuning uchun velosipedchilar va piyodalarga qatnov qismini kesib o‘tishga imkon berishingiz kerak.
answerATarget: [Piyoda]ga yo‘lni kesib o‘tishga imkon berishim kerak
answerBTarget: [Velosipedchi]ga yo‘lni kesib o‘tishga imkon berishim kerak
answerCTarget: Yo‘lni kesib o‘tayotgan harakat ishtirokchilaridan men ustunman

questionTextSource: You want to turn right here. What is correct?
explanationSource: You must let both pedestrians cross the road. They do not change their direction of movement and therefore have priority over you.
answerASource: I have to let both [pedestrians] cross the road
answerBSource: I only have to let [the oncoming pedestrian] cross the road
answerCSource: I have right of way over both [pedestrians]
questionTextTarget: Siz bu yerda o‘ngga burilmoqchisiz. Qaysi javob to‘g‘ri?
explanationTarget: Ikkala piyodani ham yo‘lni kesib o‘tishga qo‘yib yuborishingiz kerak. Ular o‘z harakat yo‘nalishini o‘zgartirmaydi va shu sabab sizdan ustunlikka ega.
answerATarget: Ikkala [piyoda]ga ham yo‘lni kesib o‘tishga imkon berishim kerak
answerBTarget: Faqat [qarshidan kelayotgan piyoda]ga yo‘lni kesib o‘tishga imkon berishim kerak

questionTextSource: What can give rise to particularly dangerous situations where several lanes turn off?
explanationSource: Drive at moderate speed into multi-lane turns. At too fast a pace you could get out of your lane and get into the lane of parallel driving vehicles. Avoid a lane change within this turning situation. There is a risk that a vehicle is directly behind you in your blind spot.
answerBSource: Changing lanes while performing the turn
questionTextTarget: Bir nechta yo‘lak buriladigan joylarda ayniqsa xavfli vaziyatlarga nima sabab bo‘lishi mumkin?
explanationTarget: Ko‘p yo‘lakli burilishlarga o‘rtacha tezlikda kiring. Juda yuqori tezlikda o‘z yo‘lagizdan chiqib ketib, parallel harakatlanayotgan transport vositalari yo‘lagiga kirib qolishingiz mumkin. Burilish jarayonida yo‘lak almashtirishdan saqlaning. Ko‘r nuqtangizda transport vositasi bevosita ortingizda bo‘lishi xavfi bor.

questionTextSource: All three vehicles want to turn left. Which is in the correct lane?
explanationSource: Only the yellow car merged into the lane correctly. He passed right, as it says on the road sign on the safety island, in order to then get in proper lane as left as possible to prepare his turn to the left. The green car is completely wrong on the rails; In this situation, the truck may only drive straight ahead or turn right.
answerBSource: The truck
questionTextTarget: Uchala transport vositasi ham chapga burilmoqchi. Qaysi biri to‘g‘ri yo‘lakda?
explanationTarget: Faqat sariq avtomobil yo‘lakka to‘g‘ri joylashgan. U xavfsizlik orolchasidagi yo‘l belgisiga muvofiq o‘ngdan o‘tib, chapga burilishga tayyorlanish uchun imkon qadar chapga to‘g‘ri joylashgan. Yashil avtomobil relslar ustida mutlaqo noto‘g‘ri joylashgan; bu vaziyatda yuk mashinasi faqat to‘g‘ri yurishi yoki o‘ngga burilishi mumkin.

questionTextSource: After turning left, you want to turn immediately right. How should you position your vehicle?
explanationSource: If you want to turn right again immediately after turning left, you get in proper lane on the right, if there are two lanes for turning left. Otherwise, keep as far to the right as possible. This is the only way you can turn right after a left turn without hindering other road users.
answerBSource: - the green car
questionTextTarget: Chapga burilgandan keyin darhol o‘ngga burilmoqchisiz. Transport vositangizni qanday joylashtirishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Agar chapga burilgandan keyin darhol yana o‘ngga burilmoqchi bo‘lsangiz va chapga burilish uchun ikki yo‘lak bo‘lsa, o‘ng yo‘lakka joylashasiz. Aks holda, imkon qadar o‘ng tomonda yuring. Faqat shunda chap burilishdan keyin boshqa yo‘l ishtirokchilariga xalaqit bermasdan o‘ngga burila olasiz.

questionTextSource: Who must signal a change of direction here?
explanationSource: The change of direction must indicate who changes it. This is true regardless of the importance of the road. So, the motorcyclist who follows the course of the turning priority road has to indicate. You continue straight ahead and do not indicate.
answerBSource: I myself
questionTextTarget: Bu yerda kim yo‘nalish o‘zgarishini signal bilan bildirishi kerak?
explanationTarget: Yo‘nalishini o‘zgartirayotgan shaxs buni signal bilan bildirishi kerak. Bu yo‘lning ahamiyatidan qat’i nazar amal qiladi. Shuning uchun buriluvchi ustun yo‘l bo‘ylab ketayotgan mototsiklchi signal berishi kerak. Siz to‘g‘ri yurishda davom etasiz va signal bermaysiz.

questionTextSource: You are on a roundabout. What should you remember?
explanationSource: In a roundabout you may only stop if this is unavoidable due to traffic, for example due to a traffic jam. Indicate when leaving the roundabout. You can only drive over paved or asphalted central islands if it is unavoidable due to the size of the vehicle. Always give priority to cyclists traveling on your right side and remaining in the roundabout.
answerASource: You can only drive over a central island if this is unavoidable because of your vehicle's size
answerBSource: You are not allowed to stop on the roadway in a roundabout
answerCSource: You must indicate to leave the roundabout
questionTextTarget: Siz aylanma yo‘ldasiz. Nimani yodda tutishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Aylanma yo‘lda faqat harakat sabab zarur bo‘lsa, masalan tirbandlik bo‘lsa, to‘xtashingiz mumkin. Aylanma yo‘ldan chiqayotganda signal bering. Qoplangan yoki asfaltlangan markaziy orolcha ustidan faqat transport vositangiz hajmi sabab buning iloji bo‘lmasa yurish mumkin. O‘ng tomoningizda harakatlanayotgan va aylanma yo‘lda qolayotgan velosipedchilarga har doim ustunlik bering.
answerATarget: Markaziy orolcha ustidan faqat transport vositangiz hajmi sabab bu muqarrar bo‘lsa yurishingiz mumkin
answerBTarget: Aylanma yo‘lda qatnov qismida to‘xtashingiz mumkin emas
answerCTarget: Aylanma yo‘ldan chiqishda signal berishingiz kerak

questionTextSource: In what situation is it especially dangerous to perform a turn?
explanationSource: When turning in poor visibility (fog), dangerous situations can arise. The same applies when turning in curves and before crests.
answerASource: Before the crest of a hill
answerBSource: Before bends in the road
questionTextTarget: Qaysi vaziyatda burilish ayniqsa xavfli?
explanationTarget: Ko‘rinish yomon bo‘lganda, masalan tumanda, burilish xavfli vaziyatlarga olib kelishi mumkin. Xuddi shuningdek, burilishlarda va tepalik cho‘qqilari oldida ham burilish xavflidir.

questionTextSource: Who is required to indicate?
explanationSource: Changing the direction of travel must always be indicated by pressing the indicator. If you stay on a turning priority road you should also indicate that. Therefore, the blue truck must indicate. You, who's turning left, must also indicate.
answerBSource: [The blue truck]
questionTextTarget: Kim signal berishi kerak?
explanationTarget: Harakat yo‘nalishini o‘zgartirish har doim burilish ko‘rsatkichini yoqish orqali bildirilishi kerak. Agar buriluvchi ustun yo‘lda qolayotgan bo‘lsangiz ham buni signal bilan ko‘rsatishingiz kerak. Shuning uchun ko‘k yuk mashinasi signal berishi kerak. Chapga burilayotgan siz ham signal berishingiz kerak.

questionTextSource: Who is required to indicate a change of driving direction here?
explanationSource: The change of the direction of travel must always be indicated early enough. All three drivers intend to turn. Therefore, all three vehicles must indicate.
answerBSource: [The red car]
questionTextTarget: Bu yerda kim haydash yo‘nalishini o‘zgartirishni signal bilan bildirishi kerak?
explanationTarget: Harakat yo‘nalishini o‘zgartirish niyati har doim yetarlicha erta signal bilan bildirilishi kerak. Uchala haydovchi ham burilmoqchi. Shuning uchun uchala transport vositasi ham signal berishi kerak.

questionTextSource: Who is required to indicate a change of driving direction here?
explanationSource: The intention to change the direction of travel must be announced at an early stage by indicating. You follow the turning priority road; this change of direction needs to be indicated.
answerBSource: The oncoming [car]
questionTextTarget: Bu yerda kim haydash yo‘nalishini o‘zgartirishni signal bilan bildirishi kerak?
explanationTarget: Harakat yo‘nalishini o‘zgartirish niyati signal berish orqali erta bildirilishi kerak. Siz buriluvchi ustun yo‘l bo‘ylab ketmoqdasiz; bu yo‘nalish o‘zgarishi signal bilan ko‘rsatilishi kerak.

questionTextSource: When do have you to indicate a change in direction?
explanationSource: If you want to turn into a T-junction or a property, you have to indicate. The same applies if you follow a turning priority road - here, too, the change of the direction of travel must be indicated.
answerASource: Before turning into a junction or a property lot
answerBSource: If I want to continue following a priority road that changes direction
answerCSource: If I want to continue straight on, thereby leaving a priority road that changes direction
questionTextTarget: Yo‘nalish o‘zgarishini qachon signal bilan bildirishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Agar T-kesishmaga yoki mulk hududiga burilmoqchi bo‘lsangiz, signal berishingiz kerak. Buriluvchi ustun yo‘l bo‘ylab ketayotganingizda ham xuddi shunday — bu yerda ham harakat yo‘nalishining o‘zgarishi signal bilan bildirilishi kerak.
answerATarget: Kesishma yoki mulk hududiga burilishdan oldin
answerBTarget: Yo‘nalishi o‘zgaradigan ustun yo‘l bo‘ylab davom etmoqchi bo‘lsam
answerCTarget: Yo‘nalishi o‘zgaradigan ustun yo‘lni tark etib to‘g‘ri yurishda davom etmoqchi bo‘lsam

questionTextSource: Why are you required to stop now?
explanationSource: Drivers who turn need to wait for vehicles from the opposite direction, even for cyclists. The red car has to wait until the green car and the cyclist are gone.
answerBSource: - green vehicle
questionTextTarget: Nega hozir to‘xtashingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Burilayotgan haydovchilar qarama-qarshi yo‘nalishdagi transport vositalarini, hatto velosipedchilarni ham kutishi kerak. Qizil avtomobil yashil avtomobil va velosipedchi o‘tib ketguncha kutishi kerak.

questionTextSource: You want to turn left. In what sequence do you prepare to take the left turn?
explanationSource: If you want to turn left, prepare the turn in the following order: 1. look, 2. indicate, 3. arrange. Looking means that you are observing follow-up traffic in the mirror and over your shoulder. Then press the indicator to the left side. Only if you can rule out danger for other road users, you may merge left in order to turn.
answerASource: Observe traffic behind you, indicate the direction of turn, enter the lane
answerBSource: Indicate the direction of turn, enter the lane, observe traffic behind you
answerCSource: Enter the lane, indicate the direction of turn, observe traffic behind you
questionTextTarget: Siz chapga burilmoqchisiz. Chap burilishga qaysi ketma-ketlikda tayyorlanasiz?
explanationTarget: Chapga burilmoqchi bo‘lsangiz, burilishga quyidagi tartibda tayyorlaning: 1. qarash, 2. signal berish, 3. yo‘lakka joylashish. Qarash degani — ortingizdagi transportni ko‘zguda va yelka oshirib kuzatish. Keyin chap burilish ko‘rsatkichini yoqasiz. Faqat boshqa yo‘l ishtirokchilari uchun xavfni istisno qila olsangiz, chapga joylashishingiz mumkin.
answerATarget: Orqadagi transportni kuzatish, burilish yo‘nalishini signal bilan bildirish, yo‘lakka joylashish
answerBTarget: Burilish yo‘nalishini signal bilan bildirish, yo‘lakka joylashish, orqadagi transportni kuzatish
answerCTarget: Yo‘lakka joylashish, burilish yo‘nalishini signal bilan bildirish, orqadagi transportni kuzatish

questionTextSource: What is the right course of action?
explanationSource: In front of the traffic sign "Stop! Give way!", colloquially also called stop sign, you must always stop. Mostly there is a stop line. The wheels must be stationary for at least a short amount of time. The cross traffic has right of way - then the cyclists on the opposite side. As a driver turning left, you have to grant him the right of way.
answerASource: I have to wait at the stop line
answerBSource: I have to allow the cyclist to pass through
answerCSource: I may make the turn in front of the cyclist
questionTextTarget: To‘g‘ri harakat qanday?
explanationTarget: “To‘xtang! Yo‘l bering!” belgisi, ya’ni odatda stop belgisi deb ataladigan belgi oldida har doim to‘xtashingiz kerak. Odatda to‘xtash chizig‘i bo‘ladi. G‘ildiraklar kamida qisqa vaqt harakatsiz turishi kerak. Kesishuvchi transport ustunlikka ega — bu yerda qarshi tomondagi velosipedchilar ham. Chapga burilayotgan haydovchi sifatida unga yo‘l berishingiz kerak.

questionTextSource: How should you behave?
explanationSource: Turning drivers generally have to wait for oncoming vehicles. These include cyclists. Cyclists who drive in their own direction on or next to the lane you also need to let pass.
answerBSource: Only give way to the cyclist travelling in the same direction
answerCSource: Sound your horn and take the turn before the two cyclists
questionTextTarget: Qanday harakat qilishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Burilayotgan haydovchilar odatda qarshidan kelayotgan transport vositalarini kutishi kerak. Bunga velosipedchilar ham kiradi. Siz bilan bir yo‘nalishda yo‘l ustida yoki yo‘l yonida harakatlanayotgan velosipedchilarni ham o‘tkazib yuborishingiz kerak.
answerBTarget: Faqat siz bilan bir yo‘nalishda ketayotgan velosipedchiga yo‘l bering
answerCTarget: Signal chalib, ikkala velosipedchidan oldin buriling

questionTextSource: You want to turn left. To whom must you give priority?
explanationSource: The oncoming traffic when turning left, has always right of way. This also applies to cyclists; they are granted priority. If pedestrians cross the street you want to turn into, the pedestrians have priority too.
answerBSource: Oncoming vehicles
answerCSource: Pedestrians seeking to cross the road into which I am turning
questionTextTarget: Siz chapga burilmoqchisiz. Kimga ustunlik berishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Chapga burilayotganda qarshidan kelayotgan transport har doim ustunlikka ega. Bu velosipedchilarga ham taalluqli; ularga ustunlik beriladi. Agar piyodalar siz burilmoqchi bo‘lgan ko‘chani kesib o‘tayotgan bo‘lsa, piyodalar ham ustunlikka ega.
answerCTarget: Men burilmoqchi bo‘lgan yo‘lni kesib o‘tmoqchi bo‘lgan piyodalarga

questionTextSource: You want to turn left. What difficulties could arise here?
explanationSource: When turning left, vehicles approaching each other have to turn in front of each other (tangent turning). This can lead to poor visibility of the straight-ahead traffic. Especially left-turning trucks can lead to massive obstructions and make it safer to turn in a wide arc (turning behind each other). There may be misunderstandings between drivers when one wants to turn behind and the other in front.
answerASource: Visibility restrictions caused by oncoming vehicles
answerBSource: Misunderstandings with oncoming traffic turning left
answerCSource: Traffic turning left can come dangerously close
questionTextTarget: Siz chapga burilmoqchisiz. Bu yerda qanday qiyinchiliklar yuzaga kelishi mumkin?
explanationTarget: Chapga burilishda bir-biriga qarab kelayotgan transport vositalari bir-birining oldidan burilishi kerak (tangent burilish). Bu to‘g‘ri kelayotgan transportni yomon ko‘rishga olib kelishi mumkin. Ayniqsa chapga burilayotgan yuk mashinalari katta ko‘rinish to‘sig‘i yaratadi va keng yoy bilan, ya’ni bir-birining ortidan burilishni xavfsizroq qilishi mumkin. Haydovchilar orasida tushunmovchiliklar bo‘lishi mumkin: biri orqadan, boshqasi oldidan burilmoqchi bo‘lsa.
answerATarget: Qarshidan kelayotgan transport vositalari sabab ko‘rinish cheklanishi
answerBTarget: Chapga burilayotgan qarama-qarshi transport bilan tushunmovchiliklar
answerCTarget: Chapga burilayotgan transport xavfli darajada yaqin kelishi mumkin

questionTextSource: Why are you required to stop now?
explanationSource: You are on a priority road and therefore you're allowed to drive before the white car coming from the right. However, as a driver who wants to turn, you have a subordinate position to traffic driving straight-ahead, in this case the tram. You have to stop.
answerBSource: Because of the cyclist
questionTextTarget: Nega hozir to‘xtashingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Siz ustun yo‘ldasiz va shuning uchun o‘ngdan kelayotgan oq avtomobildan oldin yurishingiz mumkin. Biroq burilmoqchi bo‘lgan haydovchi sifatida siz to‘g‘ri ketayotgan transportga, bu holatda tramvayga, nisbatan quyi ustunlikka egasiz. To‘xtashingiz kerak.

questionTextSource: What is the right course of action here?
explanationSource: You must not overtake the moped before the level crossing. In general, no vehicles may be overtaken at level crossings from signs 151, 156 to the rails.
answerASource: - not overtake the moped before the rail crossing
answerBSource: - overtake the moped, while allowing sufficient space to the side
answerCSource: - overtake the moped before the rail crossing once the cyclist is out of the way
questionTextTarget: Bu yerda to‘g‘ri harakat qanday?
explanationTarget: Temiryo‘l kesishmasidan oldin mopedni quvib o‘tmasligingiz kerak. Umuman olganda, 151 va 156 belgilaridan relslargacha bo‘lgan temiryo‘l kesishmalarida hech qanday transport vositasini quvib o‘tish mumkin emas.
answerATarget: - temiryo‘l kesishmasidan oldin mopedni quvib o‘tmaslik
answerBTarget: - mopedni yetarli yon masofa bilan quvib o‘tish
answerCTarget: - velosipedchi yo‘ldan o‘tgach, temiryo‘l kesishmasidan oldin mopedni quvib o‘tish

questionTextSource: Why are you required to brake now?
explanationSource: The motorcyclist comes from the left at high speed. He probably did not see you because of the oncoming vehicle. Although you have the right of way, you have to slow down, as otherwise an accident will occur.
answerBSource: Because of the van
questionTextTarget: Nega hozir tormoz berishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Mototsiklchi chap tomondan yuqori tezlikda kelmoqda. U ehtimol qarshidan kelayotgan transport vositasi sabab sizni ko‘rmagan. Siz ustunlikka ega bo‘lsangiz ham, tezlikni kamaytirishingiz kerak, aks holda avariya sodir bo‘ladi.

questionTextSource: Why do you have to wait before turning right?
explanationSource: If you want to turn, you must give priority to road users traveling straight ahead. In this case, you have lower priority to the pedestrian and the cyclist and must allow them a safe crossing.
answerBSource: Because of [the cyclist]
questionTextTarget: Nega o‘ngga burilishdan oldin kutishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Burilmoqchi bo‘lsangiz, to‘g‘ri yo‘nalishda harakatlanayotgan yo‘l ishtirokchilariga ustunlik berishingiz kerak. Bu holatda piyoda va velosipedchi sizdan ustun, ularning xavfsiz o‘tishiga imkon berishingiz kerak.

questionTextSource: What must you be aware of here?
explanationSource: Since the blue car is already in the roundabout, you have to slow down. Only when leaving the roundabout, you must indicate.
answerBSource: - give the car right of way
questionTextTarget: Bu yerda nimaga e’tibor berishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Ko‘k avtomobil allaqachon aylanma yo‘lda bo‘lgani uchun tezlikni kamaytirishingiz kerak. Faqat aylanma yo‘ldan chiqayotganda signal berishingiz kerak.

questionTextSource: You want to turn left. What driving line should you maintain?
explanationSource: Since you are on a one-way street, you don't need to fear oncoming traffic. Indicate in time. Then merge left and stay on the left lane until you reach the turn.
answerBSource: The central one
questionTextTarget: Siz chapga burilmoqchisiz. Qaysi harakat chizig‘ini saqlashingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Siz bir tomonlama ko‘chadasiz, shuning uchun qarama-qarshi transportdan xavfsirashingiz shart emas. O‘z vaqtida signal bering. Keyin chapga joylashing va burilishga yetguncha chap yo‘lakda qoling.

questionTextSource: [The cyclist] wants to continue straight ahead. What is the right course of action here?
explanationSource: Vehicles that want to turn need to let bicycles pass that drive in their direction on or next to the road, before turning. It does not matter if their wheels are motorized or not.
answerBSource: I overtake [the cyclist]
questionTextTarget: [Velosipedchi] to‘g‘ri yurishda davom etmoqchi. Bu yerda to‘g‘ri harakat qanday?
explanationTarget: Burilmoqchi bo‘lgan transport vositalari burilishdan oldin o‘z yo‘nalishida yo‘l ustida yoki yo‘l yonida harakatlanayotgan velosipedlarni o‘tkazib yuborishi kerak. Ularning velosipedi motorli yoki motorsizligi ahamiyatga ega emas.

questionTextSource: Why must you wait before taking a turn in this situation?
explanationSource: Because of the truck and the motorcycle. Here you have to pay attention to the turning rules. Wait for the vehicles to complete the maneuver and then continue turning.
answerBSource: Because of the truck
questionTextTarget: Nega bu vaziyatda burilishdan oldin kutishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Yuk mashinasi va mototsikl sabab. Bu yerda burilish qoidalariga e’tibor berishingiz kerak. Transport vositalari manevrni yakunlaguncha kuting va shundan keyin burilishni davom ettiring.

questionTextSource: Who has to indicate in this situation?
explanationSource: You and the blue car are changing direction for this reason you must indicate the change of direction.
answerBSource: [The blue car]
questionTextTarget: Bu vaziyatda kim signal berishi kerak?
explanationTarget: Siz va ko‘k avtomobil yo‘nalishni o‘zgartiryapsiz, shu sabab yo‘nalish o‘zgarishini signal bilan bildirishingiz kerak.

questionTextSource: What is the right course of action now?
explanationSource: The motorbike behind you has already changed to the left lane and will overtake you on the left. In order to merge into the left-turning lane in time, you must slow down.
answerBSource: I continue on the right-hand lane for now
answerCSource: I move without delay into the left-hand lane
questionTextTarget: Hozir to‘g‘ri harakat qanday?
explanationTarget: Ortingizdagi mototsikl allaqachon chap yo‘lakka o‘tgan va sizni chap tomondan quvib o‘tadi. Chapga burilish yo‘lagiga o‘z vaqtida kirish uchun siz tezlikni kamaytirishingiz kerak.

questionTextSource: Why are you not allowed to take the turn now?
explanationSource: At an intersection, drivers going straight ahead have the right of way. You must wait to turn until the yellow car has crossed the intersection.
answerBSource: - [red] vehicle
questionTextTarget: Nega hozir burilishingiz mumkin emas?
explanationTarget: Chorrahalarda to‘g‘ri ketayotgan haydovchilar ustunlikka ega. Sariq avtomobil chorrahadan o‘tib ketguncha burilishni kutishingiz kerak.

questionTextSource: What must you anticipate in this situation?
explanationSource: The blue car drives past the stopping bus and may want to continue straight on, even though it is in the turning lane. Since you turn left, he has right of way.
answerASource: [The blue car] will drive straight onwards
answerBSource: The [red] bus will begin to move immediately
questionTextTarget: Bu vaziyatda nimani kutishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Ko‘k avtomobil to‘xtab turgan avtobus yonidan o‘tmoqda va burilish yo‘lagida bo‘lsa ham, to‘g‘ri yurishda davom etmoqchi bo‘lishi mumkin. Siz chapga burilayotganingiz uchun u ustunlikka ega.

questionTextSource: Why are you required to stop now?
explanationSource: There is an electric scooter in the blind spot, so you must stop to avoid endangering it.
answerBSource: Because of the pedestrians
questionTextTarget: Nega hozir to‘xtashingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Ko‘r nuqtada elektr skuter bor, shuning uchun unga xavf tug‘dirmaslik uchun to‘xtashingiz kerak.

questionTextSource: What is the right course of action now?
explanationSource: The straight ahead traffic always has priority, whether the cyclist is riding on the road or on a cycle path. To make sure that no other cyclist is approaching, you must look over your shoulder to the right.
answerASource: I allow [the cyclist] to pass through
answerBSource: I glance over my right shoulder
answerCSource: I take the turn ahead of the electric scooter
questionTextTarget: Hozir to‘g‘ri harakat qanday?
explanationTarget: To‘g‘ri harakatlanayotgan transport har doim ustunlikka ega, velosipedchi yo‘lda yoki veloyo‘lakda yurganidan qat’i nazar. Boshqa velosipedchi yaqinlashmayotganiga ishonch hosil qilish uchun o‘ng yelkangiz oshirib qarashingiz kerak.

questionTextSource: Why are you required to stop now?
explanationSource: Because you want to turn left, you should wait for the tram before turning.
answerBSource: Because of [the cyclist]
questionTextTarget: Nega hozir to‘xtashingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Siz chapga burilmoqchi bo‘lganingiz uchun burilishdan oldin tramvayni kutishingiz kerak.

questionTextSource: In this situation, why do you have to wait before turning right?
explanationSource: When turning, you must give priority to longitudinal traffic, for this reason you must wait for pedestrians and cyclists to pass.
answerBSource: Because of the [cyclist]
questionTextTarget: Bu vaziyatda nega o‘ngga burilishdan oldin kutishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Burilayotganda bo‘ylama harakatga ustunlik berishingiz kerak, shu sabab piyodalar va velosipedchilar o‘tib ketguncha kutishingiz kerak.

questionTextSource: Why can you not turn off the road here?
explanationSource: The red car is still overtaking you and is travelling at a much higher speed than you. Wait for the red car to pass.
answerBSource: - [yellow] truck
questionTextTarget: Nega bu yerda yo‘ldan burilib chiqib keta olmaysiz?
explanationTarget: Qizil avtomobil hali ham sizni quvib o‘tmoqda va sizdan ancha yuqori tezlikda harakatlanmoqda. Qizil avtomobil o‘tib ketguncha kuting.

questionTextSource: In this situation, why do you have to wait before turning left?
explanationSource: You are changing the direction of travel, so you must give the tram priority.
answerBSource: Because of the [red car]
questionTextTarget: Bu vaziyatda nega chapga burilishdan oldin kutishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Siz harakat yo‘nalishini o‘zgartiryapsiz, shuning uchun tramvayga ustunlik berishingiz kerak.

questionTextSource: In this situation, why do you have to wait before turning left?
explanationSource: A motorbike is driving behind a red car; due to your change of direction, you must give the motorcyclist priority.
answerBSource: Because of the [red] car
questionTextTarget: Bu vaziyatda nega chapga burilishdan oldin kutishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Qizil avtomobil ortida mototsikl harakatlanmoqda; siz yo‘nalishni o‘zgartirayotganingiz sabab mototsiklchiga ustunlik berishingiz kerak.

questionTextSource: Why are you required to wait now?
explanationSource: The blue car and the cyclist are both going straight ahead, so they have the right of way, even if the cyclist is riding on the pavement.
answerBSource: - [blue] vehicle
questionTextTarget: Nega hozir kutishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Ko‘k avtomobil ham, velosipedchi ham to‘g‘ri ketmoqda, shuning uchun ular ustunlikka ega, hatto velosipedchi piyodalar yo‘lagida yurgan bo‘lsa ham.

questionTextSource: You want to turn out of a road with a sunken kerbstone into another road. A car is coming from the left. What applies here?
explanationSource: When there are drop kerbs (as it happens often in residential areas), the rule "Left yields to right" does not apply. Here you have to wait until you can drive onto the lane without endangering other road users. These have priority over you.
answerASource: Whoever turns into a road over a sunken kerbstone must wait
answerBSource: The rule "right before left"
answerCSource: The sunken kerbstone is of no relevance for the obligation to wait
questionTextTarget: Siz pasaytirilgan bordyurli yo‘ldan boshqa yo‘lga chiqmoqchisiz. Chap tomondan avtomobil kelmoqda. Bu yerda qanday qoida amal qiladi?
explanationTarget: Pasaytirilgan bordyurlar bo‘lganda, ko‘pincha turar joy hududlarida uchragani kabi, “o‘ngdan kelganga yo‘l ber” qoidasi amal qilmaydi. Bu yerda boshqa yo‘l ishtirokchilariga xavf tug‘dirmasdan yo‘lga chiqa olguncha kutishingiz kerak. Ular sizdan ustunlikka ega.
answerATarget: Pasaytirilgan bordyur orqali yo‘lga chiqayotgan kishi kutishi kerak
answerCTarget: Pasaytirilgan bordyurning kutish majburiyatiga aloqasi yo‘q

questionTextSource: You want to leave the [petrol station]. What is correct?
explanationSource: The blue traffic sign with the white arrow means “right direction of travel”. Here you can only turn right. Also make sure that if you want to turn onto a street from private property, you must give way to other road users such as pedestrians and vehicles.
answerASource: I have to give priority to pedestrians crossing the road
answerBSource: I can drive in both directions
answerCSource: I have right of way over vehicles coming from the left
questionTextTarget: Siz [yoqilg‘i quyish shoxobchasi]dan chiqmoqchisiz. Qaysi javob to‘g‘ri?
explanationTarget: Oq strelkali ko‘k yo‘l belgisi “majburiy harakat yo‘nalishi o‘ngga” degani. Bu yerda faqat o‘ngga burilishingiz mumkin. Shuningdek, xususiy hududdan ko‘chaga chiqayotganingizda piyodalar va transport vositalari kabi boshqa yo‘l ishtirokchilariga yo‘l berishingiz kerak.
answerATarget: Yo‘lni kesib o‘tayotgan piyodalarga ustunlik berishim kerak
answerCTarget: Chapdan kelayotgan transport vositalaridan men ustunman

questionTextSource: What must you do when leaving the edge of the roadway?
explanationSource: Anyone who wants to go from the edge of the lane into the moving traffic must make this clear by indicating. With one look in the rear-view mirror as well as over the shoulder one can merge into the lane - considering your lower priority to the moving traffic.
answerBSource: Switch on your direction indicator
answerCSource: A look into the rear-view mirror is sufficient
questionTextTarget: Qatnov qismi chetidan chiqayotganda nima qilishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Yo‘l chetidan oqimdagi harakatga qo‘shilmoqchi bo‘lgan har kim buni signal bilan aniq ko‘rsatishi kerak. Orqa ko‘zguga qarab va yelka oshirib qarab, oqimdagi harakatga nisbatan past ustunligingizni hisobga olgan holda yo‘lakka qo‘shilishingiz mumkin.

questionTextSource: You want to leave a traffic-calming zone and begin driving along a road. What should you do?
explanationSource: If you enter another street from a traffic-calmed area, you will be required to way and have to give priority to other road users. The rule "left yields to right" does not apply here! Indicate before turning and pay particular attention to cyclists and pedestrians.
answerASource: - allow pedestrians on the footpath to walk past
answerBSource: - indicate the direction of movement prior to taking the turn
questionTextTarget: Siz tinchlantirilgan harakat hududidan chiqib, yo‘l bo‘ylab harakatni boshlamoqchisiz. Nima qilishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Tinchlantirilgan harakat hududidan boshqa ko‘chaga chiqsangiz, yo‘l berishingiz va boshqa yo‘l ishtirokchilariga ustunlik berishingiz kerak. Bu yerda “o‘ngdan kelganga yo‘l ber” qoidasi amal qilmaydi! Burilishdan oldin signal bering va velosipedchilar hamda piyodalarga alohida e’tibor bering.
answerBTarget: - burilishdan oldin harakat yo‘nalishini signal bilan bildirish
answerCTarget: - “o‘ngdan kelganga yo‘l ber” qoidasiga rioya qilish

questionTextSource: You want to leave a traffic-calming zone and begin driving along a road. Cyclists are approaching from the left. Who is required to wait?
explanationSource: When entering a street from a traffic-calmed area, the "left yields to right" rule is canceled. You have to wait for pedestrians, cyclists and participants of the moving traffic.
answerBSource: The cyclists have to wait
answerCSource: Everyone is required to stop and then agree among one another who should proceed first
questionTextTarget: Siz tinchlantirilgan harakat hududidan chiqib, yo‘l bo‘ylab harakatni boshlamoqchisiz. Chap tomondan velosipedchilar yaqinlashmoqda. Kim kutishi kerak?
explanationTarget: Tinchlantirilgan harakat hududidan ko‘chaga kirayotganda “o‘ngdan kelganga yo‘l ber” qoidasi bekor qilinadi. Siz piyodalar, velosipedchilar va oqimdagi harakat ishtirokchilarini kutishingiz kerak.
answerCTarget: Hamma to‘xtab, kim birinchi yurishini o‘zaro kelishishi kerak

questionTextSource: You want to leave a property lot and turn right into a road. Cyclists are approaching from the left. Who is required to wait?
explanationSource: As a matter of principle, you have to wait at property exits and give priority to all other road users. This also applies to pedestrians on the sidewalk and cyclists. Be especially careful here.
answerBSource: The cyclists have to wait
answerCSource: Everyone is required to stop and then agree among one another who should proceed first
questionTextTarget: Siz mulk hududidan chiqib, yo‘lga o‘ngga burilmoqchisiz. Chap tomondan velosipedchilar yaqinlashmoqda. Kim kutishi kerak?
explanationTarget: Asosiy qoida sifatida, mulk hududidan chiqishda siz kutishingiz va barcha boshqa yo‘l ishtirokchilariga ustunlik berishingiz kerak. Bu piyodalar yo‘lagidagi piyodalar va velosipedchilarga ham taalluqli. Bu yerda ayniqsa ehtiyot bo‘ling.
answerCTarget: Hamma to‘xtab, kim birinchi yurishini o‘zaro kelishishi kerak

questionTextSource: You want to leave a property lot and turn into a road while passing over a footpath. What must you look out for?
explanationSource: To turn from a property over the sidewalk onto the road, you have to note: You have to wait for pedestrians on the sidewalk, cyclists on bike paths and participants in the moving traffic. You must not endanger other road users. In case of doubt, somebody must lead you into traffic.
answerASource: Pedestrians on the footpath may not be endangered
answerBSource: You should be directed by another person if necessary
answerCSource: First take note of the flowing traffic on the road
questionTextTarget: Siz mulk hududidan chiqib, piyodalar yo‘lagi ustidan o‘tib yo‘lga burilmoqchisiz. Nimaga e’tibor berishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Mulk hududidan piyodalar yo‘lagi orqali yo‘lga burilishda quyidagilarga e’tibor berishingiz kerak: piyodalar yo‘lagidagi piyodalarni, veloyo‘lakdagi velosipedchilarni va oqimdagi harakat ishtirokchilarini kutishingiz kerak. Boshqa yo‘l ishtirokchilariga xavf tug‘dirmasligingiz kerak. Shubha bo‘lsa, kimdir sizni harakat oqimiga yo‘naltirishi kerak.
answerATarget: Piyodalar yo‘lagidagi piyodalarga xavf tug‘dirmaslik kerak
answerBTarget: Zarur bo‘lsa, boshqa shaxs sizni yo‘naltirishi kerak
answerCTarget: Avvalo yo‘ldagi oqimdagi transportga e’tibor bering

questionTextSource: You want to exit the property lot. What is the right thing to do?
explanationSource: If you would like to drive out of a property exit, you have to wait for all other road users. These include both pedestrians and cyclists. If a road user renounces his or her priority, you may drive - provided that you do not endanger anyone.
answerASource: I have to wait until the pedestrians have crossed over the exit
answerBSource: I may drive on if the pedestrians waive their right of way
answerCSource: I sound my horn briefly so that the pedestrians will allow me to exit
questionTextTarget: Siz mulk hududidan chiqmoqchisiz. To‘g‘ri harakat qanday?
explanationTarget: Mulk hududi chiqishidan chiqmoqchi bo‘lsangiz, barcha boshqa yo‘l ishtirokchilarini kutishingiz kerak. Bunga piyodalar ham, velosipedchilar ham kiradi. Agar yo‘l ishtirokchisi o‘z ustunligidan voz kechsa, hech kimga xavf tug‘dirmasangiz, yurishingiz mumkin.
answerATarget: Piyodalar chiqish joyidan o‘tib ketguncha kutishim kerak
answerBTarget: Agar piyodalar o‘z ustunligidan voz kechsa, yurishim mumkin
answerCTarget: Piyodalar menga chiqishga ruxsat bersin deb qisqa signal chalaman

questionTextSource: You want to exit a parking space and move into the moving traffic. What should you do?
explanationSource: When you park out, you must not endanger other road users. Indicate that you want to drive onto the road. Look in the rear and side mirrors and make sure that no cyclist is in your blind spot by looking over your shoulder.
answerASource: I may not endanger the moving traffic
answerBSource: I have to indicate before moving off
answerCSource: I have to take note of the traffic behind me
questionTextTarget: Siz parkovka joyidan chiqib, oqimdagi harakatga qo‘shilmoqchisiz. Nima qilishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Parkovkadan chiqayotganda boshqa yo‘l ishtirokchilariga xavf tug‘dirmasligingiz kerak. Yo‘lga chiqmoqchi ekaningizni signal bilan bildiring. Orqa va yon ko‘zgularga qarang hamda yelka oshirib qarab, ko‘r nuqtangizda velosipedchi yo‘qligiga ishonch hosil qiling.
answerBTarget: Harakatni boshlashdan oldin signal berishim kerak

questionTextSource: Which are the areas you must not drive into when there is traffic congestion?
explanationSource: In the case of stagnant traffic, the following areas must be kept free for example for emergency vehicles: intersections, level crossings and cross-walks. For residential exits, this rule does not apply. If you notice that someone wants to drive off his property onto the road, you should give the driver the opportunity to enter the road.
answerBSource: Level crossings
questionTextTarget: Tirbandlik bo‘lganda qaysi joylarga kirishingiz mumkin emas?
explanationTarget: Harakat to‘xtab qolganida, masalan favqulodda xizmat transportlari uchun quyidagi joylar bo‘sh qoldirilishi kerak: chorrahalar, temiryo‘l kesishmalari va piyodalar o‘tish joylari. Turar joy chiqishlari uchun bu qoida amal qilmaydi. Agar kimdir mulk hududidan yo‘lga chiqmoqchi ekanini sezsangiz, unga yo‘lga kirish imkonini berishingiz kerak.

questionTextSource: Which are the areas you must not drive into when there is traffic congestion?
explanationSource: If traffic slows down, you may only enter intersections if you can leave them quickly. Otherwise you have to wait. Also, junctions of cross streets, cross-walks and level crossings are to be kept free. However, this does not apply to bus stops.
answerBSource: Pedestrian crossings
questionTextTarget: Tirbandlik bo‘lganda qaysi joylarga kirishingiz mumkin emas?
explanationTarget: Agar harakat sekinlashsa, kesishmaga faqat uni tezda tark eta olsangiz kirishingiz mumkin. Aks holda kutishingiz kerak. Shuningdek, ko‘ndalang ko‘chalarning qo‘shilish joylari, piyodalar o‘tish joylari va temiryo‘l kesishmalari bo‘sh qoldirilishi kerak. Biroq bu avtobus bekatlariga taalluqli emas.

questionTextSource: What do you do?
explanationSource: Due to the traffic sign you have to wait here. The red arrow in your direction indicates that priority is given to oncoming vehicles. In no case may you drive onto the sidewalk. This is reserved solely for pedestrians.
answerASource: Reduce speed and, if necessary, stop
answerBSource: Proceed, pulling over onto the pavement
answerCSource: Speed up and proceed since the oncoming traffic must wait
questionTextTarget: Nima qilasiz?
explanationTarget: Yo‘l belgisi sabab bu yerda kutishingiz kerak. Sizning yo‘nalishingizdagi qizil strelka qarshidan kelayotgan transportga ustunlik berilishini bildiradi. Hech qanday holatda piyodalar yo‘lagiga chiqishingiz mumkin emas. U faqat piyodalar uchun mo‘ljallangan.
answerATarget: Tezlikni kamaytiraman va zarur bo‘lsa to‘xtayman
answerCTarget: Tezlashib davom etaman, chunki qarshidan kelayotgan transport kutishi kerak

questionTextSource: During traffic congestion, what parts of the road infrastructure must be kept clear?
explanationSource: The pedestrian crossing (“zebra crossing”) is intended to enable pedestrians to cross the road safely and without danger. For this reason, you must leave pedestrian crossings free under all circumstances. If you drive into a junction (or crossing) in slow-moving traffic, there is a risk that you will block oncoming cross traffic or that oncoming traffic turning left will have to wait unnecessarily long. The junction or crossing would thus become even more clogged and traffic would be obstructed more than necessary.
answerBSource: Pedestrian crossings
questionTextTarget: Tirbandlik paytida yo‘l infratuzilmasining qaysi qismlari bo‘sh qoldirilishi kerak?
explanationTarget: Piyodalar o‘tish joyi (“zebra”) piyodalarga yo‘lni xavfsiz va xavfsiz tarzda kesib o‘tishga imkon berish uchun mo‘ljallangan. Shu sabab piyodalar o‘tish joylarini har qanday holatda bo‘sh qoldirishingiz kerak. Agar sekin harakatlanayotgan transportda kesishmaga kirsangiz, qarama-qarshi kesishuvchi transportni to‘sib qo‘yish yoki chapga burilayotgan qarama-qarshi transportni keraksiz uzoq kutishga majbur qilish xavfi bor. Kesishma yanada tiqilib qoladi va harakatga xalaqit beriladi.

questionTextSource: You want to continue driving straight ahead. What is correct in this situation?
explanationSource:
A group of pedestrians, cyclists or motor vehicles, which is traveling together in road traffic, is called a closed association. Closed associations must be clearly marked and must not be interrupted.
So you have to wait until all vehicles drove past you and as long as you have to wait at the stop line.
answerASource: I allow the convoy to pass through
answerBSource: I wait at the stop line
questionTextTarget: Siz to‘g‘ri yurishda davom etmoqchisiz. Bu vaziyatda qaysi javob to‘g‘ri?
explanationTarget: Yo‘l harakatida birgalikda harakatlanayotgan piyodalar, velosipedchilar yoki motorli transport vositalari guruhi yopiq kolonna deb ataladi. Yopiq kolonnalar aniq belgilanadi va ularni bo‘lib yuborish mumkin emas. Shuning uchun barcha transport vositalari yoningizdan o‘tib ketguncha kutishingiz va kutishingiz kerak bo‘lsa, to‘xtash chizig‘ida qolishingiz kerak.

questionTextSource: You want to continue driving straight ahead. What is the right course of action?
explanationSource: The car ahead blocks you in your lane. Nevertheless, you should not overtake on the right before the intersection, as this would be too dangerous. Keep your distance, reduce the speed and stop if necessary. Continue straight ahead when the free lane is free and the traffic light turns green. You can only change on the right lane if there is a sufficiently large traffic gap.
answerASource: I remain waiting in my lane
answerBSource: I move to the right if there is sufficient space in the traffic
answerCSource: I overtake the vehicle alongside me and switch lanes
questionTextTarget: Siz to‘g‘ri yurishda davom etmoqchisiz. To‘g‘ri harakat qanday?
explanationTarget: Oldingizdagi avtomobil sizning yo‘lagizni to‘sib turibdi. Shunga qaramay, chorrahadan oldin o‘ng tomondan quvib o‘tmasligingiz kerak, chunki bu juda xavfli bo‘ladi. Masofa saqlang, tezlikni kamaytiring va zarur bo‘lsa to‘xtang. Bo‘sh yo‘lak bo‘sh bo‘lganda va svetofor yashilga o‘tganda to‘g‘ri yurishda davom eting. O‘ng yo‘lakka faqat harakatda yetarlicha katta bo‘shliq bo‘lsa o‘tishingiz mumkin.
answerBTarget: Harakatda yetarli joy bo‘lsa, o‘ngga o‘taman
answerCTarget: Yonimdagi transport vositasini quvib o‘tib, yo‘lakni almashtiraman

questionTextSource: What should you do?
explanationSource: If tram rails lead through the left turn lane and you want to turn left when the tram approaches, it is important to wait with sufficient distance in front of the tram rails. When the tram passed, you can turn left.
answerASource: Stop before the left turn and keep the central divide clear
answerBSource: Turn smoothly, so as not to impede following traffic
answerCSource: Come to a stop in the central divide, where the other car is stopped
questionTextTarget: Nima qilishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Agar tramvay relslari chapga burilish yo‘lagi orqali o‘tsa va tramvay yaqinlashayotganda chapga burilmoqchi bo‘lsangiz, tramvay relslari oldida yetarli masofada kutish muhim. Tramvay o‘tib ketgach, chapga burilishingiz mumkin.
answerATarget: Chap burilishdan oldin to‘xtab, markaziy ajratmani bo‘sh qoldiraman
answerBTarget: Orqadagi transportga xalaqit bermaslik uchun silliq burilaman
answerCTarget: Boshqa avtomobil to‘xtagan markaziy ajratmada to‘xtayman

questionTextSource: What is the right course of action?
explanationSource: If a cyclist who moves to the left drives in front of you, you must not overtake him if the road is narrowed at this point and there is oncoming traffic. A safety distance of 1.5 to 2 meters could not be maintained here.
answerBSource: Accelerate
questionTextTarget: To‘g‘ri harakat qanday?
explanationTarget: Agar chapga siljiyotgan velosipedchi oldingizda harakatlansa, bu joyda yo‘l toraygan va qarama-qarshi transport bo‘lsa, uni quvib o‘tmasligingiz kerak. Bu yerda 1,5–2 metr xavfsiz masofani saqlab bo‘lmaydi.

questionTextSource: What is the right course of action?
explanationSource: On your side the road narrows. The cyclist will therefore move to the left. Because of oncoming traffic, you cannot overtake him with the required safety distance of 1.5 to 2 meters. That's why you have to wait and stay behind him.
answerBSource: Accelerate
questionTextTarget: To‘g‘ri harakat qanday?
explanationTarget: Siz tomonda yo‘l torayadi. Shu sabab velosipedchi chapga siljiydi. Qarama-qarshi transport sabab uni talab qilingan 1,5–2 metr xavfsiz masofa bilan quvib o‘ta olmaysiz. Shuning uchun kutishingiz va uning ortida qolishingiz kerak.

questionTextSource: In which situations are you not allowed to enter a junction even though the traffic light is green?
explanationSource: In the following cases, you must not enter a crossroad, even though the traffic light shows "green": with slow-moving traffic, as crossings, for example, must remain free for emergency vehicles. Secondly, when police officers regulate traffic and give instructions that deviate from the meanings of the colors of the traffic lights. In general, a green traffic light tells you that you are allowed to drive and overrules other traffic signs that regulate the right of way. Policeman orders have the highest rank and override traffic lights.
answerASource: If I would have to stop in the middle of the junction because the traffic is backing up
answerBSource: If asked to stop by a police officer
answerCSource: If the sign "Stop. Give way" is displayed at the junction
questionTextTarget: Svetofor yashil bo‘lsa ham, qaysi vaziyatlarda chorrahaga kirishingiz mumkin emas?
explanationTarget: Quyidagi hollarda svetofor “yashil” bo‘lsa ham chorrahaga kirishingiz mumkin emas: sekin harakatlanayotgan tirbandlikda, chunki chorrahalar, masalan, favqulodda xizmat transportlari uchun bo‘sh qolishi kerak. Ikkinchidan, politsiya xodimi harakatni tartibga solib, svetofor ranglari ma’nosidan farq qiluvchi ko‘rsatma berganda. Umuman olganda, yashil svetofor sizga yurishga ruxsat beradi va ustunlikni tartibga soluvchi boshqa yo‘l belgilaridan ustun turadi. Politsiya ko‘rsatmalari esa eng yuqori ustunlikka ega.
answerATarget: Agar mening yo‘nalishimdagi tirbandlik sabab chorrahaning o‘rtasida to‘xtab qolishim kerak bo‘lsa
answerCTarget: Agar chorrahada “To‘xtang. Yo‘l bering” belgisi bo‘lsa

questionTextSource: In what instances may you not enter a junction, even though you have right of way?
explanationSource: If the traffic slows down, you may enter a crossroad with a green traffic light or right of way only if you can leave it quickly without blocking it. This is particularly important in order to keep the intersection free for police, fire and ambulance emergency vehicles. If you hear a siren, you have to drive carefully to the crossroad and may only continue if you hinder no emergency vehicle. When turning left, you must slowly enter the intersection and wait there until you can drive freely without oncoming traffic.
answerASource: - would have to wait in the junction because the traffic in my direction is backing up
answerBSource: - would impede a vehicle with flashing blue lights and siren
answerCSource: - want to turn left and would have to wait in the junction because of oncoming traffic
questionTextTarget: Ustunlikka ega bo‘lsangiz ham, qaysi hollarda chorrahaga kirishingiz mumkin emas?
explanationTarget: Agar harakat sekinlashsa, yashil svetofor yoki ustunlik bo‘lsa ham chorrahaga faqat uni tezda tark eta olsangiz va to‘sib qo‘ymasangiz kirishingiz mumkin. Bu chorrahani politsiya, yong‘in xizmati va tez yordam transportlari uchun bo‘sh saqlashda ayniqsa muhim. Agar sirena eshitsangiz, chorrahaga ehtiyotkorlik bilan yaqinlashishingiz va hech qanday favqulodda transportga xalaqit bermasangizgina davom etishingiz mumkin. Chapga burilganda chorrahaga sekin kirib, qarama-qarshi transport bo‘lmaganda erkin harakatlana olguncha u yerda kutishingiz kerak.
answerATarget: - mening yo‘nalishimdagi tirbandlik sabab chorrahada kutishimga to‘g‘ri kelsa
answerBTarget: - ko‘k chiroq va sirenali transport vositasiga xalaqit bersam
answerCTarget: - chapga burilmoqchi bo‘lsam va qarama-qarshi transport sabab chorrahada kutishimga to‘g‘ri kelsa

questionTextSource: Why must you slow down here and be ready to brake?
explanationSource: You don't have to slow down because of pedestrians. He is far enough away from the constriction. The red vehicle is standing and is therefore no reason to slow down as well. However, reduce the speed because of the cyclist. He could change lanes without a hand signal and bring about a dangerous situation.
answerBSource: - [green] vehicle
questionTextTarget: Nega bu yerda tezlikni kamaytirib, tormozga tayyor turishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Piyodalar sabab sekinlashishingiz shart emas. U toraygan joydan yetarlicha uzoqda. Qizil transport vositasi to‘xtab turibdi, shuning uchun u ham sekinlashish uchun sabab emas. Biroq velosipedchi sabab tezlikni kamaytiring. U qo‘l ishorasisiz yo‘lakni almashtirib, xavfli vaziyat yaratishi mumkin.

questionTextSource: Why do you have to continue to brake?
explanationSource: A garbage truck comes towards the blue truck and forces him to stop. Since the garbage truck could swing out and get on your lane as it turns, you'll need to wait for the vehicle to be properly merged. The cyclist is no reason for braking.
answerBSource: - blue truck
questionTextTarget: Nega tormozlashda davom etishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Axlat tashuvchi mashina ko‘k yuk mashinasi tomon kelmoqda va uni to‘xtashga majbur qilmoqda. Axlat tashuvchi mashina burilayotganda keng chiqib, sizning yo‘lagizga kirishi mumkinligi sabab, transport vositasi to‘g‘ri qo‘shilib olguncha kutishingiz kerak. Velosipedchi tormozlash uchun sabab emas.

questionTextSource: You want to drive straight ahead. What should you do?
explanationSource: In case of halting traffic, you are not allowed to block any intersection or T-junction despite the green traffic light or right of way. You may need to wait at the stop line and cannot close up until the front vehicles are far enough away.
answerBSource: - between the yellow and the blue vehicle
questionTextTarget: Siz to‘g‘ri yurmoqchisiz. Nima qilishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Harakat to‘xtab qolganida, svetofor yashil bo‘lsa ham yoki siz ustunlikka ega bo‘lsangiz ham, hech bir chorraha yoki T-kesishmani to‘sib qo‘yishingiz mumkin emas. To‘xtash chizig‘ida kutishingiz kerak bo‘lishi mumkin va oldingi transport vositalari yetarlicha uzoqlashmaguncha ularga yaqinlashib ololmaysiz.
answerBTarget: - sariq va ko‘k transport vositalari orasida

questionTextSource: When may you begin driving?
explanationSource: The truck was signaled by flasher that he waives his right of way. Therefore, is after the truck driver has clearly waived with hands on his priority right and if you do not endangering the cross traffic or not substantially behinderst.
answerASource: Once the truck driver has made a hand signal clearly yielding his right of way
answerBSource: If I do not endanger or significantly impede the cross traffic
answerCSource: Immediately because the truck driver is urging me to do so
questionTextTarget: Qachon harakatni boshlashingiz mumkin?
explanationTarget: Yuk mashinasi haydovchisi chiroq ishorasi bilan ustunlik huquqidan voz kechayotganini bildirgan. Shuning uchun yuk mashinasi haydovchisi qo‘l ishorasi bilan ustunlik huquqidan aniq voz kechgandan keyin va siz kesishuvchi transportga xavf tug‘dirmasangiz yoki sezilarli xalaqit bermasangiz, harakatni boshlashingiz mumkin.
answerATarget: Yuk mashinasi haydovchisi qo‘l ishorasi bilan ustunlik huquqidan aniq voz kechgandan keyin
answerBTarget: Agar men kesishuvchi transportga xavf tug‘dirmasam yoki sezilarli xalaqit bermasam
answerCTarget: Darhol, chunki yuk mashinasi haydovchisi meni shunga undamoqda

questionTextSource: When may you take the turn?
explanationSource: As you want to turn left, oncoming traffic has priority. Only when the lorry driver gives up his right of way with a clear hand signal can you pass with due regard for oncoming traffic. Flashing your lights is not sufficient here.
answerASource: Only once the truck driver has used a hand signal to clearly indicate he is foregoing his right of way
answerBSource: Only if I do not endanger or significantly impede the cross traffic
answerCSource: Immediately, because the truck driver is prompting me to do that
questionTextTarget: Qachon burilishingiz mumkin?
explanationTarget: Siz chapga burilmoqchi bo‘lganingiz uchun qarama-qarshi transport ustunlikka ega. Faqat yuk mashinasi haydovchisi aniq qo‘l ishorasi bilan ustunlik huquqidan voz kechsa, qarama-qarshi transportga e’tibor bergan holda o‘tishingiz mumkin. Bu yerda chiroq bilan ishora qilishning o‘zi yetarli emas.
answerATarget: Faqat yuk mashinasi haydovchisi qo‘l ishorasi bilan ustunlik huquqidan voz kechayotganini aniq ko‘rsatgandan keyin
answerBTarget: Faqat men kesishuvchi transportga xavf tug‘dirmasam yoki sezilarli xalaqit bermasam
answerCTarget: Darhol, chunki yuk mashinasi haydovchisi meni shunga undamoqda

questionTextSource: You want to drive straight ahead. What should you do if the [red] truck immediately drives off on “green”?
explanationSource: You should wait here and let the truck move off, giving warning signs if necessary as it appears to be turning right. (Turn signal right)
answerASource: I wait and allow the truck to move off
answerBSource: I give a warning signal if necessary
questionTextTarget: Siz to‘g‘ri yurmoqchisiz. Agar [qizil] yuk mashinasi “yashil” chiroqda darhol yurib ketsa, nima qilishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Bu yerda kutishingiz va yuk mashinasining harakatlanishiga imkon berishingiz kerak; zarur bo‘lsa ogohlantiruvchi signal bering, chunki u o‘ngga burilayotgandek ko‘rinadi (o‘ngga burilish signali).
answerATarget: Kutaman va yuk mashinasining harakatlanishiga imkon beraman

questionTextSource: You want to drive straight ahead. Where should you position yourself in this situation?
explanationSource: The truck swerves to turn right, wait until the turn is completed. Then you can drive straight ahead.
answerASource: On the right behind the truck
answerBSource: On the left behind the truck
questionTextTarget: Siz to‘g‘ri yurmoqchisiz. Bu vaziyatda qayerda joylashishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Yuk mashinasi o‘ngga burilish uchun keng chiqmoqda, burilish yakunlanguncha kuting. Shundan keyin to‘g‘ri yurishingiz mumkin.

questionTextSource: Where is stopping prohibited?
explanationSource:
A "stop" is when a vehicle stops for less than 3 minutes for reasons unrelated to traffic without the driver getting out of the vehicle. If the driver stops for a longer time and/or leaves his vehicle, this is called parking. However, drivers are not allowed to stop everywhere so as not to endanger other road users.
Exceptions are:
- Non-stop zones
- Transitions through levels as there is a risk of collision with the train
- Narrow and incomprehensible places with a clearance width of less than 3 m
blind turns
- Hilltops
- Pedestrian crossings and the area in front of them (distance not less than 5 m), but not the area behind them.
answerBSource: On narrow sections of the road and at blind spots
questionTextTarget: Qayerda to‘xtash taqiqlanadi?
explanationTarget: “To‘xtash” — haydovchi transport vositasidan tushmasdan, transportga bog‘liq bo‘lmagan sabab bilan 3 daqiqadan kam vaqtga to‘xtashidir. Agar haydovchi uzoqroq to‘xtasa va/yoki transport vositasini tark etsa, bu parkovka deyiladi. Biroq haydovchilarga boshqa yo‘l ishtirokchilariga xavf tug‘dirmaslik uchun hamma joyda to‘xtashga ruxsat berilmaydi. Bunga quyidagilar kiradi: to‘xtash taqiqlangan hududlar, temiryo‘l kesishmalari, chunki poyezd bilan to‘qnashuv xavfi bor, o‘tish kengligi 3 metrdan kam bo‘lgan tor va noaniq joylar, ko‘rinmaydigan burilishlar, tepalik cho‘qqilari, piyodalar o‘tish joylari va ularning oldidagi hudud (kamida 5 m masofa), lekin ularning ortidagi hudud emas.
answerBTarget: Yo‘lning tor qismlarida va ko‘rinishi cheklangan joylarda

questionTextSource: Where is parking prohibited?
explanationSource: As soon as you stop or leave your vehicle for more than three minutes without any traffic-related reason, this is called parking. This is not allowed everywhere. You may not park in areas with stopping restrictions or parking prohibition - nor where you impede or obstruct other road users. Parking restrictions also apply to priority roads outside built-up areas. Use side strips or parking bays.
answerASource: At the edge of the roadway if this would prevent others from using designated parking areas
answerBSource: On priority roads outside built-up areas
answerCSource: On priority roads within built-up areas
questionTextTarget: Qayerda parkovka qilish taqiqlanadi?
explanationTarget: Transportga bog‘liq bo‘lmagan sabab bilan uch daqiqadan ko‘proq to‘xtasangiz yoki transport vositasini tark etsangiz, bu parkovka hisoblanadi. Bunga hamma joyda ruxsat berilmaydi. To‘xtash cheklangan yoki parkovka taqiqlangan joylarda, shuningdek boshqa yo‘l ishtirokchilariga xalaqit beradigan yoki to‘sqinlik qiladigan joylarda parkovka qilishingiz mumkin emas. Aholi yashash hududidan tashqaridagi ustun yo‘llarda ham parkovka cheklovlari amal qiladi. Yon chiziqlar yoki parkovka cho‘ntaklaridan foydalaning.
answerATarget: Agar bu belgilangan parkovka joylaridan boshqalar foydalanishiga to‘sqinlik qilsa, qatnov qismi chetida
answerBTarget: Aholi yashash hududidan tashqaridagi ustun yo‘llarda

questionTextSource: Where is stopping prohibited?
explanationSource:
You may only stop where you do not endanger other road users. This is the case, for example, if there is a hard shoulder separated from the carriageway by a continuous white line. 
You are not allowed to stop
- on marked lanes that are used for getting into the right of way
- on the carriageway if there is a hard shoulder
- at bus stops or other public transport stops
- at taxi ranks, so as not to block the access of taxis
- on motorways or motorways, not even on the hard shoulder.
Watch out for two-wheelers!
answerASource: On the roadway, if there is a sufficiently wide hard shoulder on the right
answerBSource: Between lane dividers when direction arrows are marked on the roadway
questionTextTarget: Qayerda to‘xtash taqiqlanadi?
explanationTarget: Faqat boshqa yo‘l ishtirokchilariga xavf tug‘dirmaydigan joyda to‘xtashingiz mumkin. Masalan, qatnov qismidan uzluksiz oq chiziq bilan ajratilgan yo‘l cheti yo‘lagi bo‘lsa. Quyidagi joylarda to‘xtash mumkin emas: ustun yo‘lga kirish uchun mo‘ljallangan belgilangan yo‘laklarda, o‘ngda yetarli yo‘l cheti yo‘lagi bo‘lsa qatnov qismida, avtobus bekatlari yoki boshqa jamoat transporti bekatlarida, taksilar kirishini to‘smaslik uchun taksi turargohlarida, avtomagistrallarda va shunga o‘xshash yo‘llarda, hatto yo‘l cheti yo‘lagida ham. Ikki g‘ildirakli transportlarga e’tibor bering!
answerATarget: O‘ngda yetarlicha keng yo‘l cheti yo‘lagi bo‘lsa, qatnov qismida
answerBTarget: Qatnov qismida yo‘nalish strelkalari belgilangan bo‘lsa, yo‘lak ajratgichlari orasida

questionTextSource: Where is stopping prohibited?
explanationSource: On and up to five meters in front of a pedestrian crossing, you must not stop because you obstruct the clear view of other drivers on pedestrians. These could then be recognized too late. After a pedestrian crossing you may stop. Waiting is allowed on manhole covers, but not parking.
answerASource: On pedestrian crossings and up to 5 m before pedestrian crossings
answerBSource: Immediately behind pedestrian crossings
answerCSource: Over manholes and other road covers
questionTextTarget: Qayerda to‘xtash taqiqlanadi?
explanationTarget: Piyodalar o‘tish joyida va undan 5 metrgacha oldinda to‘xtamasligingiz kerak, chunki boshqa haydovchilarning piyodalarni aniq ko‘rishini to‘sib qo‘yasiz. Natijada piyodalar juda kech sezilishi mumkin. Piyodalar o‘tish joyidan keyin to‘xtashingiz mumkin. Lyuk qopqoqlari ustida kutishga ruxsat beriladi, lekin parkovka qilishga emas.
answerATarget: Piyodalar o‘tish joylarida va ulardan 5 m oldingacha
answerCTarget: Lyuklar va boshqa yo‘l qopqoqlari ustida

questionTextSource: Where is parking prohibited?
explanationSource: You are not allowed to park at the edge of the road, where you block marked parking areas and thus hinder other road users. Parking is also prohibited in front of lowered curbs, which are for example located in front of driveways. Lowered curbs should allow wheelchair users to cross the road. Safe and therefore allowed is parking after a pedestrian crossing.
answerASource: At the edge of the roadway if this would prevent others from using designated parking areas
answerBSource: Before sunken kerbstones
questionTextTarget: Qayerda parkovka qilish taqiqlanadi?
explanationTarget: Belgilangan parkovka joylarini to‘sib, boshqa yo‘l ishtirokchilariga xalaqit beradigan yo‘l chetida parkovka qilishingiz mumkin emas. Shuningdek, masalan kirish yo‘llari oldida joylashgan pasaytirilgan bordyurlar oldida ham parkovka taqiqlanadi. Pasaytirilgan bordyurlar nogironlar aravachasidagi odamlarga yo‘lni kesib o‘tishga imkon berishi kerak. Piyodalar o‘tish joyidan keyin parkovka qilish xavfsiz va shuning uchun ruxsat etilgan.
answerATarget: Agar bu belgilangan parkovka joylaridan boshqalar foydalanishiga to‘sqinlik qilsa, qatnov qismi chetida

questionTextSource: Where are you allowed to park on the left in the direction of travel?
explanationSource: You may only park in the direction of travel on the right-hand side of the road. This also applies if all parking spaces are on the left side. You may have to turn if allowed. The only exception are one-way streets, as they are only used in one direction of travel. If there are rails on the right, you must also park on the left, but always observe parking restrictions.
answerBSource: Where there are rails on the right-hand side
answerCSource: Where parking is prohibited on the right
questionTextTarget: Harakat yo‘nalishi bo‘yicha chap tomonda qayerda parkovka qilishga ruxsat beriladi?
explanationTarget: Odatda faqat harakat yo‘nalishi bo‘yicha yo‘lning o‘ng tomonida parkovka qilishingiz mumkin. Bu barcha parkovka joylari chap tomonda bo‘lsa ham amal qiladi. Ruxsat berilgan bo‘lsa, qayrilib olishingiz kerak bo‘lishi mumkin. Faqat bir tomonlama ko‘chalar istisno, chunki ulardan faqat bitta harakat yo‘nalishida foydalaniladi. Agar o‘ng tomonda relslar bo‘lsa, chap tomonda ham parkovka qilishingiz kerak, ammo parkovka cheklovlariga doimo rioya qiling.

questionTextSource: Who is parked?
explanationSource: If the car driver deliberately stops the vehicle and stays there for more than three minutes or leaves the stopped vehicle, this is considered parking. The stopping and waiting in front of a gate is not considered parking, no matter how long it takes, because the break is due to traffic.
answerASource: Anyone who stops for more than 3 minutes
answerBSource: Anyone who leaves his vehicle
answerCSource: Anyone who waits longer than 3 minutes at a closed level crossing barrier
questionTextTarget: Kim parkovka qilgan hisoblanadi?
explanationTarget: Agar avtomobil haydovchisi transport vositasini ataylab to‘xtatib, u yerda uch daqiqadan ko‘proq qolsa yoki to‘xtagan transport vositasini tark etsa, bu parkovka hisoblanadi. Darvoza oldida to‘xtash va kutish qancha davom etishidan qat’i nazar parkovka hisoblanmaydi, chunki to‘xtash transport harakati sababli bo‘ladi.
answerBTarget: Transport vositasini tark etgan har kim
answerCTarget: Yopiq temiryo‘l shlagbaumi oldida 3 daqiqadan ko‘proq kutgan har kim

questionTextSource: Which vehicles are allowed to stop in the second row alongside other vehicles?
explanationSource: Even with the hazard lights on, waiting in the second row is only permitted for taxis. They count to public transport. Exceptions are tolerated if you let an old or handicapped person get off or on.
answerASource: Taxis, to let passengers board or alight, traffic conditions permitting
answerBSource: Any vehicle, provided the hazard warning lights are switched on
questionTextTarget: Qaysi transport vositalariga boshqa transport vositalari yonida ikkinchi qatorda to‘xtashga ruxsat beriladi?
explanationTarget: Avariya chiroqlari yoqilgan bo‘lsa ham, ikkinchi qatorda kutishga faqat taksilarga ruxsat beriladi. Ular jamoat transporti hisoblanadi. Keksa yoki nogiron odamni tushirish yoki chiqarish uchun istisnolar toqat qilinishi mumkin.
answerATarget: Taksilar, yo‘lovchilarni chiqarish yoki tushirish uchun, agar harakat sharoiti imkon bersa
answerBTarget: Avariya chiroqlari yoqilgan bo‘lsa, har qanday transport vositasi

questionTextSource: Who has stopped wrongly?
explanationSource: On the hard shoulder, the right vehicle is allowed to wait. However, it's not permitted to wait on the left of the solid line. The left vehicle stops illegally.
answerBSource: [The light-coloured car]
questionTextTarget: Kim noto‘g‘ri to‘xtagan?
explanationTarget: Yo‘l cheti yo‘lagida o‘ngdagi transport vositasiga kutishga ruxsat beriladi. Biroq uzluksiz chiziqning chap tomonida kutishga ruxsat yo‘q. Chapdagi transport vositasi noqonuniy to‘xtagan.

questionTextSource: Who is wrongly parked?
explanationSource: The zigzagged line shortens or extends a waiting or parking ban. As far as there is no border marking on the road, a parking ban of up to 15 meters in front of and behind the stop sign applies. In this situation, the white car parked incorrectly because it stands on the marking. The dark car, however, parks properly.
answerBSource: The [dark-coloured] car
questionTextTarget: Kim noto‘g‘ri parkovka qilgan?
explanationTarget: Zigzag chiziq kutish yoki parkovka taqiqini qisqartiradi yoki uzaytiradi. Yo‘lda chegara belgisi bo‘lmasa, bekat belgisi oldidan va ortidan 15 metrgacha parkovka taqiqi amal qiladi. Bu vaziyatda oq avtomobil noto‘g‘ri parkovka qilgan, chunki u belgi ustida turibdi. To‘q rangli avtomobil esa to‘g‘ri parkovka qilgan.

questionTextSource: When are you not allowed to park on the right-hand lane?
explanationSource: In general, parking is prohibited if it could endanger other road users. Visual disability is also included. Therefore, parking is not allowed in all three situations.
answerASource: If there is an adequately paved hard shoulder to the right of the road lane
answerBSource: If there are rails integrated into the road lane
answerCSource: If there is a suitable parking lane to the right of the road lane
questionTextTarget: Qachon o‘ng yo‘lakda parkovka qilishingiz mumkin emas?
explanationTarget: Umuman olganda, boshqa yo‘l ishtirokchilariga xavf tug‘dirishi mumkin bo‘lsa, parkovka taqiqlanadi. Ko‘rinishni to‘sish ham bunga kiradi. Shuning uchun uchala vaziyatda ham parkovka qilish mumkin emas.
answerATarget: Yo‘l yo‘lagining o‘ng tomonida yetarlicha mustahkamlangan yo‘l cheti yo‘lagi bo‘lsa
answerCTarget: Yo‘l yo‘lagining o‘ng tomonida mos parkovka yo‘lagi bo‘lsa

questionTextSource: What must you be aware of here?
explanationSource: Never wait on the left of the lane mark. You would impede or even endanger subsequent vehicles. The side strip is designed for parking, waiting and slow vehicles. As a slow-moving driver you must use such a sufficiently wide and well-fastened strip for driving. In this case, you may drive over the thick solid line with your vehicle representing an official lane boundary.
answerASource: It is not permitted to stop to the left of the boundary line of the carriageway
answerBSource: Slower moving vehicles must use the hard shoulder if possible
answerCSource: Driving across the boundary line of the carriageway is prohibited at all times
questionTextTarget: Bu yerda nimaga e’tibor berishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Yo‘lak belgisi chap tomonida hech qachon kutmang. Siz keyingi transport vositalariga xalaqit berishingiz yoki hatto xavf tug‘dirishingiz mumkin. Yon chiziq parkovka, kutish va sekin transport vositalari uchun mo‘ljallangan. Sekin harakatlanuvchi haydovchi sifatida yetarlicha keng va mustahkam bunday chiziqdan haydash uchun foydalanishingiz kerak. Bu holda rasmiy yo‘lak chegarasini bildiruvchi qalin uzluksiz chiziqdan transport vositangiz bilan o‘tishingiz mumkin.
answerATarget: Qatnov qismining chegara chizig‘ining chap tomonida to‘xtashga ruxsat berilmaydi
answerBTarget: Sekin harakatlanuvchi transport vositalari imkon bo‘lsa yo‘l cheti yo‘lagidan foydalanishi kerak
answerCTarget: Qatnov qismi chegara chizig‘idan o‘tish har doim taqiqlangan

questionTextSource: What vehicles may be parked alongside other parked cars on the roadway?
explanationSource: In exceptional cases, taxis are only allowed to stop in the second row when passengers are getting on or off.
answerASource: All taxis when passengers get in or out
answerBSource: All vehicles when they are being loaded or unloaded
answerCSource: All vehicles when they have their hazard lights switched on
questionTextTarget: Qaysi transport vositalari yo‘lda parkovka qilingan boshqa avtomobillar yonida to‘xtashi mumkin?
explanationTarget: Istisno hollarda, taksilarga faqat yo‘lovchilar chiqayotgan yoki tushayotgan paytda ikkinchi qatorda to‘xtashga ruxsat beriladi.
answerATarget: Yo‘lovchilar chiqayotganda yoki tushayotganda barcha taksilar
answerBTarget: Yuk ortilayotgan yoki tushirilayotgan barcha transport vositalari
answerCTarget: Avariya chiroqlari yoqilgan barcha transport vositalari

questionTextSource: Where is it forbidden to stop?
explanationSource: You are not allowed to wait on acceleration or on deceleration lanes. Also, on lanes with directional arrows you must not stop. This does not apply to driveways in front of properties.
answerBSource: On lanes marked with direction arrows
answerCSource: In front of entrances to and exits from property lots
questionTextTarget: Qayerda to‘xtash taqiqlangan?
explanationTarget: Tezlanish yoki sekinlashish yo‘laklarida kutishingiz mumkin emas. Shuningdek, yo‘nalish strelkalari belgilangan yo‘laklarda ham to‘xtamasligingiz kerak. Bu mulklar oldidagi kirish yo‘llariga taalluqli emas.
answerCTarget: Mulk hududlariga kirish va chiqish joylari oldida

questionTextSource: Who has stopped incorrectly?
explanationSource: On pedestrian crossings and five meters before, waiting is prohibited. The aim of this provision is to ensure a clear view of approaching vehicles and pedestrians who are willing to cross in this traffic situation.
answerBSource: The [yellow car]
questionTextTarget: Kim noto‘g‘ri to‘xtagan?
explanationTarget: Piyodalar o‘tish joylarida va ulardan besh metr oldin kutish taqiqlanadi. Bu qoidaning maqsadi yaqinlashayotgan transport vositalari va o‘tmoqchi bo‘lgan piyodalarni aniq ko‘rishni ta’minlashdir.

questionTextSource: Where may you park here?
explanationSource: In one-way streets you're only allowed to drive in one direction. If you have a side strip on the left and right, you may park both left and right.
answerASource: - on the left and right-hand hard shoulders
answerBSource: - on the left-hand hard shoulder only
answerCSource: - on the right-hand hard shoulder only
questionTextTarget: Bu yerda qayerda parkovka qilishingiz mumkin?
explanationTarget: Bir tomonlama ko‘chalarda faqat bitta yo‘nalishda harakatlanishga ruxsat beriladi. Agar chap va o‘ng tomonda yon chiziq bo‘lsa, chapda ham, o‘ngda ham parkovka qilishingiz mumkin.
answerATarget: - chap va o‘ng tomondagi yo‘l cheti yo‘laklarida
answerBTarget: - faqat chap tomondagi yo‘l cheti yo‘lagida
answerCTarget: - faqat o‘ng tomondagi yo‘l cheti yo‘lagida

questionTextSource: Where is it forbidden to stop?
explanationSource: Stopping occurs when a vehicle is stopped for less than 3 minutes for non-traffic reasons while the driver does not leave the vehicle. If the driver stops longer and/or leaves his vehicle, this is referred to as parking. You are not allowed to stop everywhere, for example so as not to endanger other road users. Fire brigade access roads and no-stopping zones, among other things, are excluded; a distinction is made here between parking and stopping. This does not apply to all one-way streets.
answerASource: In front of fire service access roads
answerBSource: On any one-way road
questionTextTarget: Qayerda to‘xtash taqiqlangan?
explanationTarget: Haydovchi transport vositasini tark etmasdan, transportga bog‘liq bo‘lmagan sabab bilan 3 daqiqadan kam to‘xtasa, bu to‘xtash hisoblanadi. Agar haydovchi uzoqroq to‘xtasa va/yoki transport vositasini tark etsa, bu parkovka deb ataladi. Hamma joyda to‘xtashga ruxsat berilmaydi, masalan boshqa yo‘l ishtirokchilariga xavf tug‘dirmaslik uchun. Yong‘in xizmati kirish yo‘llari va to‘xtash taqiqlangan hududlar bundan mustasno; bu yerda parkovka va to‘xtash farqlanadi. Bu barcha bir tomonlama ko‘chalarga taalluqli emas.

questionTextSource: What must you reckon with when you see a hazard warning light flash?
explanationSource: A driver must exercise special caution when perceiving a hazard light. Drivers of a car can use this for a variety of reasons. One example are regular buses that want to allow children to get in and out. Also, when a vehicle is towed, both the towed car and the towing vehicle will turn on their hazard light. A breakdown is also indicated by pressing the warning light and coming to a stop as soon as possible on the edge of the road.
answerASource: - a school bus with children alighting
answerBSource: - a car being towed
questionTextTarget: Avariya chirog‘i yonib-o‘chayotganini ko‘rsangiz, nimani kutishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Haydovchi avariya chirog‘ini ko‘rganda alohida ehtiyotkorlik ko‘rsatishi kerak. Avtomobil haydovchilari uni turli sabablarga ko‘ra yoqishi mumkin. Masalan, bolalarni tushirish yoki chiqarish uchun muntazam avtobuslar. Transport vositasi tortib ketilayotganda tortilayotgan avtomobil ham, tortayotgan transport vositasi ham avariya chiroqlarini yoqadi. Buzilish ham avariya chirog‘ini yoqish va imkon qadar tez yo‘l chetida to‘xtash orqali ko‘rsatiladi.

questionTextSource: What should you do if you see a vehicle with its hazard lights on?
explanationSource: If the hazard warning lights are switched on, there can be many reasons and causes. To be on the safe side, you should reduce your speed and drive past the vehicle with extra attention.
answerBSource: I ready myself for hazards occurring suddenly
answerCSource: I continue driving just as before
questionTextTarget: Avariya chiroqlari yoqilgan transport vositasini ko‘rsangiz, nima qilishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Avariya chiroqlari yoqilgan bo‘lsa, buning ko‘plab sabablari bo‘lishi mumkin. Xavfsizlik uchun tezlikni kamaytirib, transport vositasi yonidan alohida e’tibor bilan o‘ting.
answerBTarget: To‘satdan yuzaga kelishi mumkin bo‘lgan xavflarga tayyorlanaman

questionTextSource: For which purposes is a horn allowed to be used outside built-up areas?
explanationSource: You may announce your intention to overtake by horns or by flash light. You may also use a horn or flash light to warn other road uses and alert them of hazards. The operation of the horn as a call sign, without a specific danger situation, is not allowed.
answerBSource: As an overtaking signal
questionTextTarget: Aholi yashash hududidan tashqarida signal qaysi maqsadlarda ishlatilishi mumkin?
